quotes.text: Our board of directors unanimously agreed to proceed with certain initial steps related to exploring our strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.
our board of directors decided to engage in a process to consider strategic alternatives.
Merrill Lynch to act as our financial advisor in connection with reviewing our strategic alternatives.
We entered into a confidentiality agreement with New Mountain Capital on March 19, 2014.
we executed confidentiality agreements with a number of the potential buyers.
twenty-five potential buyers executed confidentiality agreements with us, received a copy of the marketing materials and/or met with the Company's management.
five parties, comprising four financial buyers and one strategic buyer, submitted preliminary and non-binding indications of interest.

two additional parties, comprising one financial party ("Party X") and one strategic party ("Party Y"), contacted representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch on an unsolicited basis to inquire about the process.
Party X submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest to BofA Merrill Lynch
Party X informed representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch that it was no longer interested in pursuing a potential transaction with the Company.
Party Y submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest

five of the remaining six interested parties communicated to representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch that they were unable to proceed with the process due to concerns regarding valuation and, in some cases, the interested parties' own internal initiatives and strategic priorities. The sixth remaining interested party declined to respond to BofA Merrill Lynch's communications regarding a potential transaction.
our board of directors decided to terminate the process to explore potential strategic alternatives at that time
early February 2015, New Mountain Capital contacted representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch and requested a meeting.
On February 10, 2015, New Mountain Capital met with representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch and expressed its interest in discussions with the Company regarding a potential transaction.
On February 19, 2015, New Mountain Capital delivered an unsolicited indication of interest to the Company to acquire us for a per share price of $19.25.

On February 26, 2015, New Mountain Capital delivered a revised indication of interest reflecting an increased per share price of $20.05, and indicated that this was the highest price it was willing to offer.
March 13, 2015, New Mountain Capital communicated that the $20.05 per share offer price was New Mountain Capital's "best and final" offer.
March 29, 2015, we received a revised draft of the merger agreement from New Mountain Capital.
the parties executed the merger agreement and the related transaction documents
issued a press release announcing the transaction on the morning of April 8, 2015.

events.summary: The board agreed to begin exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.
The board decided to engage in a process to consider strategic alternatives.
BofA Merrill Lynch was engaged as the Company's financial advisor for the strategic alternatives review.
New Mountain Capital executed a confidentiality agreement with the Company.
The tracked unnamed 2014 bidder cohort had executed confidentiality agreements and received sale-process materials by the first-round deadline.
notes: Represents the unnamed bidders later captured in the April 14 preliminary indication group.

events.summary: Four unnamed financial buyers and one unnamed strategic buyer submitted preliminary and non-binding indications of interest ranging from $20.00 to $22.00 per share.
notes: New Mountain Capital is excluded because the filing states that it decided not to submit a preliminary indication of interest in the 2014 cycle.

events.summary: Party X and Party Y contacted BofA Merrill Lynch on an unsolicited basis to inquire about the process.
Party X submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest at $21.50 to $23.00 per share.
Party X withdrew from the process before receiving data-room access or meeting with management.
events.drop_reason_text: Party X informed representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch that it was no longer interested in pursuing a potential transaction with the Company.

events.summary: Party Y submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest at $19.50 to $20.50 per share.
The remaining 2014 bidders communicated that they could not proceed because of valuation concerns or stopped responding to BofA Merrill Lynch.
events.drop_reason_text: five of the remaining six interested parties communicated to representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch that they were unable to proceed with the process due to concerns regarding valuation and, in some cases, the interested parties' own internal initiatives and strategic priorities. The sixth remaining interested party declined to respond to BofA Merrill Lynch's communications regarding a potential transaction.
notes: Party X is excluded because it had already withdrawn on May 14, 2014.

events.summary: The board terminated the 2014 strategic alternatives process.
events.boundary_note: Board terminated the 2014 process because of limited buyer interest and operational uncertainty after the Marietta facility fire.

events.summary: New Mountain Capital reopened sale discussions after the 2014 process had been terminated.
events.boundary_note: New Mountain Capital's unsolicited re-engagement marked the start of a new process cycle.

events.summary: New Mountain Capital met with BofA Merrill Lynch and expressed interest in a potential transaction.
New Mountain Capital delivered an unsolicited indication of interest at $19.25 per share.
notes: The filing says the indication of interest was supported by a highly confident financing letter.

events.summary: New Mountain Capital delivered a revised indication of interest at $20.05 per share and said that was the highest price it was willing to offer.
New Mountain Capital stated that $20.05 per share was its best and final offer.
notes: The filing also says New Mountain Capital requested an exclusivity extension while working toward a definitive merger agreement.

events.summary: New Mountain Capital returned a revised draft merger agreement.
notes: A revised draft merger agreement is a formal proposal signal.

events.summary: The parties executed the merger agreement and related transaction documents.
notes: The merger agreement summary outside the chronology states that each outstanding share would be converted into the right to receive $20.05 in cash.

events.summary: The parties issued a press release announcing the transaction.

exclusions.explanation: New Mountain Capital received the first-round process letter but decided not to submit a preliminary indication of interest, so it is excluded from the April 14, 2014 grouped proposal cohort.
The March 27, 2014 process letter set a first-round preliminary-indication deadline, not a final-round milestone within the event taxonomy.
The May 22, 2014 distribution of a draft merger agreement to the six remaining bidders lacked a stated final-round announcement or deadline, so it is not separately extracted as a round milestone.
The February 27, 2015 exclusivity agreement extended the existing confidentiality and standstill provisions; it is not a new sale-process NDA event.
The March 18, 2015 draft merger agreement sent to New Mountain Capital was a target-side negotiation step, not a bidder proposal event.
